questions.question: In a Makefile, what is a 'target'?
options: A variable that stores compiler flags
The file or action that a rule builds
questions.explanation: A target is what a rule produces (usually a file). Format: 'target: prerequisites\n\trecipe'. Make checks if the target is older than its prerequisites; if so, it runs the recipe.

questions.question: What does this Makefile rule produce when you run 'make hello'?
questions.explanation: $@ = target name (hello), $< = first prerequisite (hello.c). The rule expands to: gcc -Wall -O2 -o hello hello.c. This compiles hello.c into an executable called 'hello'.

questions.question: Make recipe lines MUST be indented with a TAB character — spaces will cause an error.
questions.explanation: This is Make's most infamous requirement: recipe lines MUST start with a hard TAB (\t). Spaces cause 'missing separator' errors. This dates to 1976 and has never been changed for backward compatibility.

questions.question: The special target .PHONY in a Makefile declares targets that are not actual ___.
questions.explanation: .PHONY: clean all test — tells Make these targets don't correspond to files. Without .PHONY, if a file named 'clean' exists, 'make clean' would say 'clean is up to date' and skip the recipe.

questions.question: This Makefile has a subtle bug causing parallel builds to fail. Find it:
questions.code:
SRCS = main.c utils.c parser.c
OBJS = $(SRCS:.c=.o)

app: $(OBJS)
	$(CC) -o $@ $^

parser.o: parser.c parser.h
	$(CC) -c $<

utils.o: utils.c parser.h
	$(CC) -c $<

main.o: main.c utils.h
	$(CC) -c $<

parser.h: parser_gen
	./parser_gen > parser.h
options: utils.o depends on parser.h which is generated — but Make might try to compile utils.c before parser.h is generated in parallel (-j)
questions.explanation: In parallel builds (-j4), Make may start compiling utils.o and parser.o simultaneously. But both depend on parser.h which must be generated first. The dependency exists but in parallel, generation and compilation may race. Fix: ensure all .o rules explicitly depend on parser.h.

questions.question: Match each Make automatic variable to its meaning:
questions.explanation: $@ = target (what we're building). $< = first dependency (source file in compilation). $^ = all dependencies (for linking). $* = the '%' match in pattern rules (%.o: %.c → $* is the filename stem).
